repo_prior_art.cite: none; doc/API.md §3 records only seed ExactID/PartialID catalogues and no network-exposure entry

published_prior_art.cite: Manski1990 gives the generic bounded-outcome partial-identification logic; SchroderOprescuFeuerriegelKallus2026 gives sharp exposure-mapping misspecification bounds on a known network, but not graph-completion ADE projection

repo_prior_art.cite: none; closest catalogue material is unrelated panel partial-DID and seed PartialID infrastructure, not shared hidden-edge graph completion

published_prior_art.cite: none; closest SchroderOprescuFeuerriegelKallus2026 assumes known network and propensity-ratio exposure sensitivity, while ZhangMohanPearl2023 treats linear graphical model uncertainty without product-vs-shared completion gap

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no in-catalogue graph-completion certificate proposal found

published_prior_art.cite: classical matching/set-covering polyhedral certificates are prior art for the certificate mechanics; no cited causal paper gives the ADE graph-completion application

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The certificate claim leans on capacitated b-matching, odd-set, and lifted-cover inequalities but the related work omits the classical polyhedral prior art, e.g. Edmonds 1965 matching polytope and Wolsey-style cover/lifting results.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Observable-law matching alternates between matching the full conditional distribution of Y and matching conditional means; the finite LP claim is well-posed only after locking P to the finite vector of observed conditional means or adding latent outcome distributions.

soundness_flags.label: No hidden-effect collapse
soundness_flags.one_line: The joint set collapses when Y_i(a,s,0)=Y_i(a,s,1), but the cellwise comparator still allows U_i(a,s,0) != U_i(a,s,1), so equality of joint and cellwise identified sets does not follow unless the no-hidden-effect restriction is imposed on U as well.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The certificate system only encodes demanded z_i(a)=1 events; graph-realizability of endpoints can also require z_i(a)=0, which needs upper/no-treated-neighbor constraints such as sum_e x_e a_j = 0.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Rewrite Assumption law-matching and Theorem 1 to use a named observed conditional-mean vector, or explicitly introduce latent outcome distributions if full-law matching is intended.
Repair the no-hidden-effect sanity corollary by either restricting the cellwise comparator to U_i(a,s,0)=U_i(a,s,1) or weakening the claim to joint-set collapse only.
Add zero-exposure incompatibility constraints to Conjecture 2's certificate formulation and state the certificate over signed demand pairs z=1 and z=0.

Add a short polyhedral-combinatorics paragraph comparing the certificate conjecture to matching-polytope odd-set inequalities and lifted cover inequalities, then isolate what is causal rather than standard IP theory.
Include the four-unit capacity witness as an explicit numerical table of assignments, demanded events, and feasible edge vectors so Conjecture 1's strict-containment route is auditable.

recommended_next_step: Patch the mean-vs-law target, repair the no-hidden-effect corollary, and redraft Conjecture 2 with both positive and zero exposure-event constraints plus classical polyhedral citations.
